title: Product Context
summary: Structured product context for agents. Fill sections from repo evidence and user intent; do not leave examples as final content.

sections.heading: Product Summary
sections.purpose: Name the product, who it serves, and the value it provides.

example.name: Project name
example.summary: A concise description of the product behavior this repo implements.
operatingModel: CLI, service, library, web app, or deployed worker

sections.body:
- What the product is:
- Who it serves:
- Primary value proposition:
- Deployment or operating model:

sections.heading: Users And Personas
sections.purpose: Capture humans, systems, operators, and permission boundaries that shape workflows.

accessBoundaries: Tenant, role, API key, or local-only boundary

sections.body:
- Primary users:
- Secondary users:
- Operators, administrators, or integrators:
- Permissions, tenancy, or access boundaries:

sections.heading: Core Workflows
sections.purpose: List the product workflows agents must preserve when planning changes.

coreWorkflows: Create or ingest a domain object

sections.body:
- Workflow 1:
- Workflow 2:
- Setup, onboarding, or provisioning flow:
- Review, audit, support, or recovery flow:

sections.heading: Domain Model
sections.purpose: Identify domain entities, relationships, lifecycles, and identifiers found in code/docs.

relationships: A job belongs to a queue

sections.body:
- Core entities:
- Important relationships:
- State machines or lifecycle stages:
- Identifiers, keys, scopes, or hierarchy rules:

sections.heading: Product Invariants
sections.purpose: Record stable behaviors, safety guarantees, and compatibility expectations.

invariants: A completed record is not processed twice
nonGoals: Do not replace the external system of record

sections.body:
- Behaviors that must remain stable:
- Security, privacy, or compliance guarantees:
- Determinism, compatibility, or migration guarantees:
- Performance or availability expectations:

sections.heading: Architecture Boundaries
sections.purpose: Show module/service/API ownership and generated contracts that must stay synchronized.

contracts: OpenAPI file, SQL schema, SDK fixture, generated artifact

sections.body:
- Public trust boundaries:
- Service or module ownership:
- API, SDK, CLI, or UI responsibilities:
- Generated artifacts or contracts that must stay in sync:

sections.heading: Data And Integrations
sections.purpose: Map data stores, schemas, external systems, and observability requirements.

integrations: Payment provider, queue, auth service, SDK

sections.body:
- Primary data stores and ownership:
- External services or provider dependencies:
- Event, analytics, audit, or observability expectations:
- Data retention, privacy, or redaction rules:

sections.heading: Quality And Release Expectations
sections.purpose: Capture test surfaces, commands, docs, migrations, and rollback concerns.
sections.body:
- Test surfaces that protect product behavior:
- Documentation, API contract, or SDK fixture expectations:
- Rollout, migration, compatibility, or rollback requirements:

sections.heading: Open Questions
sections.purpose: List assumptions that need human confirmation instead of inventing answers.

risks: Unclear ownership of generated contract files

sections.body:
- Product assumptions that still need confirmation:
- Known risks or decisions to revisit:
